verses.text: Micah's word concerns Samaria and Jerusalem; let all the earth hear, for the Lord is witness against them. A solemn summons. God Himself testifies against His people's sin.
Micah's word concerns Samaria and Jerusalem; let all the earth hear, for the Lord is witness against them. A solemn summons. God Himself testifies against His people's sin.
The LORD comes forth from His place; the mountains melt under Him 'as wax before the fire.' Awful descent. All creation yields before the coming of God.
The LORD comes forth from His place; the mountains melt under Him 'as wax before the fire.' Awful descent. All creation yields before the coming of God.
All this is for the sin of Jacob; Samaria shall be made a heap, and her idols beaten to pieces. Idolatry judged. The capital of sin is laid in ruins.
All this is for the sin of Jacob; Samaria shall be made a heap, and her idols beaten to pieces. Idolatry judged. The capital of sin is laid in ruins.
All this is for the sin of Jacob; Samaria shall be made a heap, and her idols beaten to pieces. Idolatry judged. The capital of sin is laid in ruins.
Micah wails, for the wound is incurable and has reached even to Jerusalem. Spreading plague. Sin's contagion passes from one city to another.
Micah wails, for the wound is incurable and has reached even to Jerusalem. Spreading plague. Sin's contagion passes from one city to another.
He names the doomed cities one by one; evil 'came down from the LORD unto the gate of Jerusalem.' Judgment at the door. The stroke advances to the very capital.
He names the doomed cities one by one; evil 'came down from the LORD unto the gate of Jerusalem.' Judgment at the door. The stroke advances to the very capital.
He names the doomed cities one by one; evil 'came down from the LORD unto the gate of Jerusalem.' Judgment at the door. The stroke advances to the very capital.
Lachish was 'the beginning of the sin' to Zion; mourn for the children gone into captivity. Mourning called for. The land must grieve the loss its sin has brought.
